behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/range-resolver/function-evaluator handoff, not just a single pure function. A unit-only SUM test can miss bugs in `A1:A10` reference expansion. Rejected System because behavior text is only in-memory formula evaluation (`=SUM(A1:A10)`), with no OS/network/subprocess dependency. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML step is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook with numeric values in A1..A10, set formula cell to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run normal engine evaluation path, assert computed value equals arithmetic sum and that range endpoints are included.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at module boundary between dependency tracking and evaluation order across multiple cells. Single-function unit tests of arithmetic won’t catch cascaded reference propagation failures. Rejected System because behavior text is in-memory cross-cell calculation only. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Build in-memory sheet: `A1=3`, `B1==A1*2`, `C1==B1+1`; evaluate workbook; assert `B1=6` and `C1=7` via standard dependency graph execution path.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection failure emerges when graph construction and evaluator interact; unit-testing one helper may miss runtime cycle handling. Rejected System because behavior text specifies logical dependency detection only, not OS/process/network behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML output dependency exists.
behaviors.test_description: In-memory sheet with `A1==B1+1` and `B1==A1+1`; run evaluator with normal dependency resolution; assert explicit circular-reference error is returned and evaluation terminates.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often in lookup-range extraction + function execution + error mapping interaction. A pure unit test of matching logic can miss table-range addressing bugs. Rejected System because behavior text is formula-level lookup semantics only. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because this is deterministic function logic, no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory table range with known key/value pairs, evaluate formula using exact-match VLOOKUP for (a) present key and (b) missing key, and assert correct value vs not-found error code through full formula engine path.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: This behavior is primarily deterministic expression semantics of nested IF branching; minimum failure boundary can be one evaluator object/function with no external I/O. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff beyond local expression evaluation. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because no real infra or AI dependency is described.
behaviors.test_description: At evaluator-unit boundary, feed parsed expression (or formula string if parser is inside same object boundary) and test `A1` values 5, 15, 25 to assert outputs `low`, `medium`, `high`.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: Potential failures usually come from cell-value retrieval + function argument normalization interaction (empty vs blank handling), not just string join logic. Rejected System because behavior text is in-memory function behavior only. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model output quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Set up in-memory cells with mixed strings and empties, evaluate CONCATENATE formula with separator, assert output omits empty-cell contributions and separator placement is correct.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-sheet reference resolver + workbook/sheet namespace wiring. Unit tests of arithmetic alone miss inter-sheet lookup bugs. Rejected System because behavior text covers workbook-internal resolution, not OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI dependency exists.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2, set `Sheet2!A1=10`, set `Sheet1!B1==Sheet2!A1+1`, evaluate and assert `Sheet1!B1=11` through normal resolver path.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: This is dependency-graph invalidation and scheduler ordering across modules; a local unit of one formula cannot catch propagation/order bugs. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/process/network effects. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because recalculation is deterministic engine logic with no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: In-memory dependency chain/diamond (e.g., A1 -> B1,C1 -> D1), evaluate once, mutate A1, trigger recalc, assert updated values and that no stale intermediate values remain (implying correct topological recomputation).

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at boundary between edit history (undo/redo stack) and recalculation state restoration across dependent cells. Unit-testing stack operations alone misses recompute/state-sync defects. Rejected System because behavior text does not require disk/process/network. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML dependency exists.
behaviors.test_description: In-memory workbook: create dependency chain, evaluate, edit upstream formula to change downstream values, run undo, assert restored formula text and restored dependent computed values match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real filesystem persistence and binary serialization/deserialization boundaries (`to disk and loading it back`), where mocks can hide truncation/encoding/flush issues. Rejected Integration because in-process fake storage cannot fully reproduce real file I/O behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML output dependency is present.
behaviors.test_description: Write workbook with representative formulas and floating-point edge values to a real temp file using production serializer, reload via production loader, assert exact formula text equality and numeric values within strict precision policy (or exact bit-level where required).
